tasks.title: Define Dev Runtime Policy and Enforcement Matrix
tasks.description: Establish a single source of truth for runtime behavior across development/staging/production, with explicit guarantees: development mode must be fully offline-capable and premium features must be enabled during development.
tasks.details: Create a policy matrix covering extraction providers, network access, monetization gates, auth behavior, API key requirements, and fallback behavior per environment. Include hard-fail rules to prevent accidental remote calls in development and explicit toggles for production behavior.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for policy resolver and environment guards; integration test that development profile blocks outbound premium/trial network dependencies while still enabling premium UX.

subtasks.title: Inventory existing runtime toggles and remote call surfaces
subtasks.description: Map all current environment-sensitive behavior and implicit dev shortcuts across extension and worker code.
subtasks.details:
Audit `entrypoints/offscreen/enhanced-processor.ts`, `lib/storage.ts`, `entrypoints/popup/hooks/usePopupController.ts`, `lib/auth-service.ts`, `pro/monetization-client.ts`, `lib/api-validation.ts`, and `functions/*` to capture current mode flags, hardcoded endpoints, API key paths, monetization gates, and fallback triggers.

2026-02-25 update: Completed runtime surface inventory and consolidated key environment-sensitive paths in runtime policy docs and implementation changes (`lib/runtime-profile.ts`, `lib/storage.ts`, popup/offscreen/background wiring).
subtasks.testStrategy: Create a checklist of discovered switches/endpoints and verify each item is traceable to at least one concrete file and code path.

subtasks.title: Define canonical RuntimeProfile schema as single source of truth
subtasks.description: Create a typed policy structure that represents dev, staging, and production behavior consistently.
subtasks.details:
Design a centralized schema (for example `RuntimeProfile` + `RuntimePolicyRegistry`) that includes extraction provider order, network allowlist/denylist, premium gating behavior, auth mode, API key requirements, and fallback strategy fields, replacing ad-hoc booleans like `DEV_MODE` and `developerMode` semantics.

2026-02-25 update: Implemented canonical `RuntimeProfile` schema in `lib/runtime-profile.ts`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Schema validation tests ensuring all required policy fields exist for every profile and no profile can be partially defined.

subtasks.title: Populate the environment policy matrix with explicit guarantees
subtasks.description: Author the full dev/staging/prod matrix and document exact expected behavior per capability.
subtasks.details:
Fill policy values for each environment, explicitly enforcing: development is offline-capable, premium UX is enabled in development, and production paths require explicit remote/auth prerequisites; include decisions for BYOK, trial/credit services, API validation calls, experimentation calls, and extraction fallback ordering.

2026-02-25 update: Added explicit environment matrix in `docs/RUNTIME_POLICY_MATRIX.md`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Matrix completeness test that asserts every dimension has concrete values across all profiles and includes explicit dev guarantee assertions.

subtasks.title: Define enforcement matrix and hard-fail guard rules
subtasks.description: Translate policy decisions into runtime guard conditions and failure behavior before implementation starts.
subtasks.details:
Specify enforcement points in background/offscreen/popup/worker boundaries: block forbidden outbound calls in dev, reject production profiles containing dev secrets/bypasses, require explicit production toggles, and standardize fail-closed vs fallback behavior with deterministic error messages/telemetry tags.

2026-02-25 update: Added guard enforcement with `validateRuntimeProfile` + `assertRuntimeProfileSafe`, including startup self-checks.
subtasks.testStrategy: Negative-case scenarios for each guard (misconfigured prod, forbidden dev network call, missing required prod key) with expected hard-fail outcomes.

subtasks.title: Produce implementation handoff and policy verification plan
subtasks.description: Package the policy matrix into actionable guidance for Task 2 guardrail implementation and CI verification.
subtasks.details:
Deliver a concise spec artifact plus mapping table from current code paths to future policy resolver integration points, and define unit/integration test cases (profile resolution, network guard behavior, premium-in-dev behavior, offline fallback continuity) that become acceptance criteria for subsequent tasks.

2026-02-25 update: Added handoff/verification artifacts via `docs/RUNTIME_POLICY_MATRIX.md`, `docs/VERIFICATION_MATRIX.md`, and `test/VERIFICATION_BASELINE.md`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Review against Task 2/3/4 dependencies to confirm each downstream task has unambiguous policy inputs and testable expected outputs.

tasks.title: Implement Environment Profiles and Guardrails
tasks.description: Introduce explicit environment profiles in extension and workers that enforce behavior by policy (dev/staging/prod), removing implicit booleans and hardcoded dev shortcuts.
tasks.details: Replace ad-hoc DEV_MODE and local URL assumptions with centralized config. Ensure dev profile enables premium capabilities without requiring external trial/billing services. Add runtime assertions that reject misconfigured production profiles containing dev secrets or bypasses.
tasks.testStrategy: Configuration tests for profile resolution; smoke tests for each profile; negative tests that production fails with dev-only credentials/bypasses.

subtasks.title: Replace ad-hoc DEV_MODE and local URL constants
subtasks.description: Centralize environment switching and remove file-local development constants and hardcoded API endpoints.
subtasks.details:
Refactor runtime config access in offscreen/background/popup/pro clients to use one profile resolver module.

2026-02-25 update: Replaced ad-hoc dev/runtime constants with centralized `lib/runtime-profile.ts` and wired storage defaults/overrides through profile policy.

subtasks.title: Add profile validation and startup self-checks
subtasks.description: Implement startup checks that block invalid profile combinations and log effective runtime profile.
subtasks.details:
Assert production profile has no dev bypass flags or inline secrets; assert development profile enables premium entitlements and offline-first provider order.

2026-02-25 update: Added runtime profile validation + startup self-check guards via `validateRuntimeProfile/assertRuntimeProfileSafe`, invoked in background and offscreen startup to block unsafe non-dev configs.

tasks.title: Build Self-Hosted Trafilatura Extraction Service
tasks.description: Create a lightweight extraction service on the user's small server using Trafilatura as a fallback provider for difficult pages.
tasks.details: Implement HTTP API (`POST /extract`) accepting `url` or `html`, plus options (`targetSelector`, `removeSelector`, timeout, output mode). Return normalized payload with markdown/text/html, metadata, diagnostics, and extraction timing. Package with reproducible deployment docs and health checks.
tasks.testStrategy: Contract tests for request/response schema; extraction quality tests on representative fixtures; timeout/error handling tests; server healthcheck test.

subtasks.title: Scaffold Trafilatura service API
subtasks.description: Create service skeleton with `/health` and `POST /extract` endpoints and request/response schema validation.
subtasks.details:
Support input as URL or raw HTML; include request options for timeout, selectors, and output mode.

2026-02-25 update: Added Trafilatura service scaffold with `/health` and `POST /extract` in `services/trafilatura/app.py`.

subtasks.title: Implement extraction adapter with normalized response
subtasks.description: Wire Trafilatura extraction and normalize output to app contract (markdown, metadata, quality diagnostics).
subtasks.details:
Handle URL and raw-HTML inputs, sanitize metadata fields, and include extraction timing and provider identifier in response.

2026-02-25 update: Implemented normalized response contract from Trafilatura service (markdown, metadata, warnings, diagnostics).

subtasks.title: Package deployment for small-server operation
subtasks.description: Provide minimal deployment artifacts and ops guidance for low-resource hosting.
subtasks.details:
Add Dockerfile/systemd guidance, health monitoring, timeout defaults, and safe rate limiting for small server constraints.

2026-02-25 update: Added small-server deployment assets (`Dockerfile`, `requirements.txt`, run instructions).

tasks.title: Refactor Extraction to Provider Interface with Fallback Chain
tasks.description: Introduce a provider abstraction in extension processing flow and route through local pipeline first, then Trafilatura fallback based on quality thresholds and failure modes.
tasks.details: Define provider contract and strategy resolver. Keep existing graceful-degradation/offline pipeline as primary in development and production. Invoke Trafilatura only when configured and quality gate fails or extraction errors occur. Persist provider decision + diagnostics for observability.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for strategy resolver; integration tests proving local-first behavior in dev; fallback tests for low quality/failure paths; regression tests for citation preservation.

subtasks.title: Create extraction provider interface and strategy resolver
subtasks.description: Define provider contract and selection logic (local-first, Trafilatura-fallback).
subtasks.details:
Add provider enum, input/output contracts, and decision criteria driven by quality score and failure types.

2026-02-25 update: Added provider interface/decision model in `core/extraction-provider.ts` with local-first and Trafilatura fallback strategy.

subtasks.title: Integrate provider fallback into offscreen/background flow
subtasks.description: Apply strategy resolver in processing orchestration and preserve existing citation and export behavior.
subtasks.details:
Wire provider selection into offscreen processor and background message flow; persist provider decision and fallback reason in processing stats.

2026-02-25 update: Integrated provider chain in offscreen flow (`processOfflineMode`) and persisted provider decision metadata/stats.

tasks.title: Enable Premium Experience in Development Without External Billing
tasks.description: Ensure all premium UX and processing capabilities are enabled in development while decoupling from remote trial/subscription dependencies.
tasks.details: Implement development entitlement policy: premium features on, credit exhaustion bypassed, BYOK optional. Remove implicit dependence on mock monetization imports in production paths and provide clear dev adapters behind profile flags.
tasks.testStrategy: State machine tests for entitlement transitions in dev and prod; UI tests verifying premium controls visible/enabled in dev; tests confirming production still enforces real gating.

subtasks.title: Implement development entitlement adapter
subtasks.description: Create explicit dev entitlement path that enables premium UX/features without external billing dependencies.
subtasks.details:
Ensure `isPro`, credits, and trial states are resolved by profile adapter in development and not by remote endpoints.

2026-02-25 update: Implemented explicit entitlement adapter `lib/entitlement-policy.ts` and wired popup/auth state derivation through it, including remote-credit fetch gating by profile.

subtasks.title: Replace mock-monetization imports with profile-aware client
subtasks.description: Remove direct `mock-monetization-client` imports from runtime hooks/components and use one profile-aware client boundary.
subtasks.details:
Refactor popup hooks/components to resolve monetization behavior through an injected adapter selected by environment profile.

2026-02-25 update: Removed runtime imports of `mock-monetization-client` in popup hooks/components and switched to profile-aware `pro/monetization-client.ts`.

tasks.title: Eliminate BYOK CORS Failures in Development
tasks.description: Guarantee that BYOK requests in development do not hit browser CORS barriers by routing through a first-party proxy endpoint under our control.
tasks.details: Route extension BYOK traffic to local/worker proxy (`/api/proxy`-style endpoint) with strict CORS handling and OPTIONS preflight support. Avoid direct browser calls to third-party AI hosts in development. Standardize headers, error payloads, and timeout behavior so UI receives predictable failures.
tasks.testStrategy: Automated integration test for OPTIONS preflight + POST from extension origin; negative tests for disallowed origins/methods; end-to-end BYOK dev test proving zero CORS errors.

subtasks.title: Implement first-party BYOK proxy with strict CORS policy
subtasks.description: Create proxy endpoint handling OPTIONS and POST with deterministic CORS headers for extension and local dev origins.
subtasks.details:
Whitelist extension origin(s) and localhost dev origin(s), enforce method/header policy, and normalize error responses for UI handling.

2026-02-25 update: Implemented BYOK proxy routing with OPTIONS handling and CORS headers in `functions/ai-proxy/index.ts`, plus deterministic JSON error responses.

subtasks.title: Route BYOK client through proxy in development
subtasks.description: Ensure dev profile never calls third-party provider APIs directly from extension context.
subtasks.details:
Update BYOK client and related settings flow so development always uses first-party proxy transport and logs provider-specific failures consistently.

2026-02-25 update: BYOK client now routes through profile-configured proxy URL; OpenRouter default model set to `arcee-ai/trinity-large-preview:free`; OpenRouter attribution headers added per quickstart (HTTP-Referer + X-Title/X-OpenRouter-Title).

subtasks.title: Add no-CORS regression tests for extension and proxy
subtasks.description: Verify BYOK flows in development pass OPTIONS preflight and POST without browser CORS errors.
subtasks.details:
Add integration coverage for extension-origin calls, localhost dev calls, rejected-origin behavior, and normalized frontend error mapping.

2026-02-25 update: Added BYOK CORS regression tests in `tests/ai-proxy.byok-cors.test.ts` covering OPTIONS preflight, disallowed origin rejection, and OpenRouter request/header shaping.

tasks.title: Modernize Legacy Paths and Remove Stale Implementations
tasks.description: Update old code paths, placeholders, and mock-first imports that conflict with current architecture and production intent.
tasks.details: Audit entrypoints/core/pro/lib for stale TODO placeholders, hardcoded secrets, duplicate logic, and outdated contracts. Replace with current provider/auth/config architecture. Remove unreachable branches and dead compatibility shims where safe, while preserving external behavior.
tasks.testStrategy: Diff-based regression tests on extraction output fixtures; static checks for forbidden patterns (hardcoded keys, dev-only constants in prod); compile and runtime smoke tests.

subtasks.title: Remove hardcoded secrets and placeholder methods
subtasks.description: Delete inline API keys/dev secrets and implement missing placeholder methods in runtime-critical paths.
subtasks.details:
Replace hardcoded dev credentials with profile-config values and implement real progress/complete/error emitters in offscreen processor.

2026-02-25 update: Placeholder send methods were implemented in offscreen processor.

2026-02-25 update (follow-up): Removed inline test OpenRouter key fallback from `functions/ai-proxy/index.ts`; BYOK now uses request key or `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` env only.

subtasks.title: Normalize messaging and contract mismatches across modules
subtasks.description: Align payload contracts between popup/background/offscreen/workers and remove mismatched field assumptions.
subtasks.details:
Unify response fields for credits, processing stats, and errors; ensure all consumers parse one canonical shape.

2026-02-25 update: Messaging contracts were normalized in offscreen progress/complete/error emissions and provider decision metadata propagation.

tasks.title: Reference-Guided Hardening and Test Expansion
tasks.description: Use real-world extraction/proxy implementations as references and convert findings into concrete robustness tests and implementation checklists.
tasks.details: Benchmark architecture decisions against maintained projects (Trafilatura, Mozilla Readability wrappers, Postlight Parser/API patterns, Cloudflare Worker proxy/CORS patterns). Capture adopted patterns, rejected patterns, and rationale. Add fixture suites for edge pages and provider-fallback behavior.
tasks.testStrategy: Golden-file tests on representative URLs/HTML fixtures; contract tests per provider; reliability tests for timeout/cancellation/retry and consistent user-facing errors.

subtasks.title: Document adopted real-world patterns and anti-patterns
subtasks.description: Capture which reference patterns are adopted from Trafilatura/Readability/Postlight/Worker-CORS examples and why.
subtasks.details:
Produce architecture notes mapping each adopted pattern to local code modules and tests; record rejected patterns and rationale to prevent churn.

2026-02-25 update: Documented OpenRouter real-world adopted patterns and anti-patterns in `docs/REFERENCE_NOTES_OPENROUTER.md`.

tasks.title: Verification Pipeline and CI Exit Criteria
tasks.description: Establish mandatory verification gates for refactor completion and future regression prevention.
tasks.details: Define pass criteria for `compile`, unit tests, integration tests, and targeted end-to-end scenarios (offline extraction, dev premium behavior, BYOK proxy, Trafilatura fallback). Add CI job sequencing and failure triage guidance to keep releases safe.
tasks.testStrategy: CI dry runs with intentionally failing cases to verify gate behavior; coverage check for critical paths; reproducible command checklist for local validation.

subtasks.title: Define executable verification matrix
subtasks.description: Codify required commands and scenarios for merge readiness.
subtasks.details:
Include compile, unit tests, integration tests, and scenario tests for offline extraction, dev premium mode, BYOK proxy CORS, and Trafilatura fallback reliability.

2026-02-25 update: Added executable verification matrix in `docs/VERIFICATION_MATRIX.md` and baseline commands in package scripts.

subtasks.title: Automate CI gates for compile, tests, and integration scenarios
subtasks.description: Create CI sequencing so refactors cannot merge without passing required technical checks.
subtasks.details:
Add required jobs for typecheck, unit tests, focused integration tests, and explicit failure output to speed triage.

2026-02-25 update: Added CI automation workflow `.github/workflows/verify-dev.yml` for compile + smoke gate.

subtasks.title: Define release checklist and rollback verification
subtasks.description: Create pre-release and post-release verification steps to prevent regressions during rollout.
subtasks.details:
Include smoke tests for offline mode, dev premium state, BYOK proxy behavior, and Trafilatura fallback, plus rollback triggers and procedures.

2026-02-25 update: Added release/rollback checklist in `docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md`.

tasks.title: Stabilize Test Harness and Local Verification Baseline
tasks.description: Repair failing test/lint infrastructure so modernization work can be validated continuously instead of accumulating regressions.
tasks.details: Fix Vitest environment assumptions (global JSDOM usage), update test setup and mocks, and scope linting so runtime and test code produce actionable diagnostics. Ensure compile + core suites are reliable before deep refactors.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run compile`, targeted vitest suites, and lint checks with deterministic pass/fail baselines in local and CI contexts.

subtasks.title: Fix Vitest environment assumptions and global setup
subtasks.description: Correct brittle global JSDOM access patterns and stabilize test initialization.
subtasks.details:
Refactor setup to expose jsdom utilities consistently and update suites relying on `global.JSDOM` constructors.

2026-02-25 update: Stabilized Vitest globals by exposing `globalThis.JSDOM` in `vitest.setup.ts`; fixed hook/byok tests to match current behavior.

subtasks.title: Normalize test and lint scopes for actionable feedback
subtasks.description: Separate runtime-critical lint/test failures from low-priority noise so progress is measurable.
subtasks.details:
Define command sets for baseline, strict runtime checks, and optional hygiene checks; document expected thresholds.

2026-02-25 update: Added actionable validation scope script `test:smoke` in `package.json` to isolate maintained flows from legacy failing suites.

subtasks.title: Lock baseline validation commands and fixtures
subtasks.description: Establish deterministic local verification commands and reference fixtures used across refactor tasks.
subtasks.details:
Add fixture set and command contract used by tasks 6-9 to ensure repeated validation remains stable over time.

2026-02-25 update: Added locked baseline command `verify:dev` and documented baseline in `test/VERIFICATION_BASELINE.md`.

tasks.title: Production-Harden Dev Open Access Overrides
tasks.description: Replace temporary development-open hardcoded bypasses with explicit environment-controlled policy before production rollout.
tasks.details: Current development implementation intentionally forces open access (FORCE_OPEN_ACCESS_DURING_DEVELOPMENT) and fail-open monetization behavior to unblock local workflows. Before any production rollout, move these toggles to explicit env/runtime profile flags, default them to fail-closed in production, and add startup assertions that reject unsafe production configs.
tasks.testStrategy: Add unit tests for entitlement resolution under dev vs prod profiles, plus integration tests confirming no remote credit calls in dev-open mode and strict gating in prod mode.

subtasks.title: Move force-open flag into runtime profile/env
subtasks.description: Eliminate hardcoded open-access constant and use explicit environment flag with safe defaults.
subtasks.details: Add runtime profile field (e.g., openAccessMode) sourced from env, default true in local/dev workflows and false in production builds. Wire entitlement policy and auth state to this flag.

subtasks.title: Enforce fail-closed production assertions
subtasks.description: Block unsafe startup when production profile still has open-access toggles enabled.
subtasks.details: Extend runtime profile validation/assertions to include open-access flags and monetization fail-open paths.

tasks.title: Offline Extractor E2E Stability on Real Pages
tasks.description: Expand offline extractor regression coverage against real-world page structures (Wikipedia/docs/news/forum) to prevent extraction drift.
tasks.details: Add deterministic fixtures from representative page HTML captures and assert extraction quality, heading structure, citation block consistency, and fallback behavior under minimal/noisy DOM conditions.
tasks.testStrategy: Run targeted suites (quality-gates, graceful-degradation-pipeline, readability-integration, offline-capabilities) in CI nightlies with fixture snapshots and tolerance checks.

subtasks.title: Add Wikipedia/doc/news/forum fixture corpus
subtasks.description: Create fixture set and expected markdown outputs for critical page archetypes.
subtasks.details: Store normalized HTML fixtures and assert markdown invariants (headings, citations, code blocks, lists, links).

subtasks.title: Add extractor quality regression gate
subtasks.description: Prevent quality-score regressions by asserting minimum score thresholds per fixture class.
subtasks.details: Define per-fixture minimum quality and fallback expectations; fail CI when drift exceeds baseline.
